property.name: message
property.value: Inputs should not reference checkstyle's own files

property.name: format
property.value: ^// non-compiled with javac: (Compilable with Java(\d+)|(contains specially crafted set of imports)|(but was compiled on jdk before)|(missing package)|(package statement is different than folder)|(reference to non existen))

property.name: message
property.value: Violation comments should not be placed inside Javadoc.

property.name: query
property.value: //*[self::METHOD_DEF or self::CTOR_DEF or self::COMPACT_CTOR_DEF or self::CLASS_DEF or self::INTERFACE_DEF or self::ENUM_DEF or self::ANNOTATION_DEF or self::RECORD_DEF or self::ANNOTATION_FIELD_DEF or self::ENUM_CONSTANT_DEF]/MODIFIERS/SINGLE_LINE_COMMENT[ ./COMMENT_CONTENT[contains(@text, 'violation')] and preceding-sibling::ANNOTATION]

message.value: Violation comment must not be placed between an annotation and its target. Place it before the annotation block instead.

property.name: query
property.value: //*[self::METHOD_DEF or self::CTOR_DEF or self::COMPACT_CTOR_DEF or self::CLASS_DEF or self::INTERFACE_DEF or self::ENUM_DEF or self::ANNOTATION_DEF or self::RECORD_DEF or self::ANNOTATION_FIELD_DEF or self::ENUM_CONSTANT_DEF]/MODIFIERS/SINGLE_LINE_COMMENT[ ./COMMENT_CONTENT[contains(@text, 'violation')] and preceding-sibling::BLOCK_COMMENT_BEGIN[ ./COMMENT_CONTENT[starts-with(@text, '*')]]]

message.value: Violation comment must not be placed between a Javadoc block and its target. Place it before the Javadoc block instead.
'// ok' comments should have an explanation, or they are unnecessary.
